items.title: Hasnol Zam Zam Appointed New Health Ministry Sec-Gen
items.summary: Hasnol Zam Zam has been transferred from Mosti to MOH as secretary-general effective March 9, replacing Suriani Ahmad, amid several leadership changes in other ministries. The post Hasnol Zam Zam Appointed New Health Ministry Sec-Gen appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Coronary Stent Excluded From Civil Servants’ GL Coverage In Public Hospitals: UiTM
items.summary: UiTM says coronary stents are not covered under civil servants’ guarantee letters (GL) for treatment in UiTM Hospital, in line with procedures in MOH hospitals. Stents are categorised as special devices, for which applicants can request financial aid. The post Coronary Stent Excluded From Civil Servants’ GL Coverage In Public Hospitals: UiTM appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Why Children Have Rights, And Older Persons Still Rely On ‘Kindness’
items.summary: If Malaysia is serious about preparing for the “grey wave”, it must move beyond well-meaning policies and begin building a rights-based legal architecture for ageing. The post Why Children Have Rights, And Older Persons Still Rely On ‘Kindness’ appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Age Caps Are Blocking Experienced Doctors From Specialist Training — Medical Officer
items.summary: The public health system needs more specialists, yet rigid age limits block long-serving doctors from specialist training, even if self-funded. The policy risks wasting experienced talent and undermining efforts to expand the public sector specialist workforce. The post Age Caps Are Blocking Experienced Doctors From Specialist Training — Medical Officer appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Pharmacist Contract Delays Trigger Talent Loss To Private Sector, Senator Warns
items.summary: Senator RA Lingeshwaran says delays in permanent appointments are pushing pharmacists out of MOH. Of 352 reserve candidates awaiting posts, 209 have left. He warned MOH cannot keep a “wait for vacancies” hiring model if it drives talent to the private sector. The post Pharmacist Contract Delays Trigger Talent Loss To Private Sector, Senator Warns appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: MSNMMI Launches MyNuCLEaR Platform To Map Nuclear Medicine Services In Malaysia
items.summary: The Malaysian Society of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging (MSNMMI) has launched MyNuCLEaR, an online platform mapping nuclear medicine facilities nationwide. The portal aims to improve access to services, information, and therapies such as theranostics. The post MSNMMI Launches MyNuCLEaR Platform To Map Nuclear Medicine Services In Malaysia appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Master’s Graduates Run Hospitals On ‘Study Leave’ But Can’t Apply For Subspecialty Training — Physician
items.summary: A requirement for three consecutive full-year LNPT reports may delay subspecialty training for Master’s graduates by several years. One doctor says this is because Master’s training is classified as “study leave”, despite trainees continuing to run wards, clinics, and on-calls. The post Master’s Graduates Run Hospitals On ‘Study Leave’ But Can’t Apply For Subspecialty Training — Physician appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Undermining India’s Primary Health Care Missions
items.summary: Instead of a thrust on key areas such as women and children’s health, the government has chosen to expand tertiary health care. The post Undermining India’s Primary Health Care Missions appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Why Malaysia Must Use AI To Strengthen TB Control — Mohamad Arif Awang Nawi
items.summary: A USM senior lecturer urges Malaysia to use artificial intelligence to strengthen tuberculosis control. AI could detect TB earlier, predict hotspots, and improve treatment adherence, though challenges like fragmented data and limited capacity remain. The post Why Malaysia Must Use AI To Strengthen TB Control — Mohamad Arif Awang Nawi appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Teen Pregnancy Data Is Incomplete Without Abortion Figures — Dr SP Choong
items.summary: Dr SP Choong argues teen pregnancy data cited in a Ministry of Health report is misleading because it counts only hospital births, not abortions. He says the issue must also address access to safe, legal abortion and accurate information. The post Teen Pregnancy Data Is Incomplete Without Abortion Figures — Dr SP Choong appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Female trainee doctor found dead in Kuala Krai dorm
items.summary: KOTA BARU: A female trainee doctor was found dead in a dormitory in Sultan Ismail Petra Hospital in Kuala Krai on Friday (March 7). Read full story
